so_claims.claim_text: Plaintiff Andrew J. Pigors is a Michigan resident and lawful titleholder of a manufactured home at Lot 17, 1977 Whitehall Rd, Muskegon County, Michigan.
Defendant Shady Oaks Park MHP LLC is, upon information and belief, a Michigan limited liability company operating the Shady Oaks community in Muskegon County.
Defendant Shady Oaks MHP LLC is a distinct Michigan limited liability company whose name and filings were used in related eviction filings.
Defendant Homes of America, LLC (“HOA”) manages and/or operates manufactured housing communities including Shady Oaks and acted through on‑site personnel during events alleged herein.

Defendant Partridge Equity Group and Defendant Alden Global Capital exercised ownership and/or control over HOA and/or the park entities in an enterprise structure to shield liability while directing operations.
Defendant Henry Brandel is a neighbor and participant in the removal of Plaintiff’s property during the July 17, 2025 set‑out.
Venue is proper in Muskegon County under MCL 600.1621 and 600.1605. The amount in controversy exceeds the jurisdictional minimum of this Court.

From late 2024 through mid‑2025, raw sewage was present near Plaintiff’s lot; EGLE issued violations. Despite this, Defendants continued billing utilities without remediation credits and failed to timely respond (≈31–34 days).

Defendants refused to accept or properly credit valid payments (including MDHHS and TrueNorth assistance), misapplied payments, and layered unauthorized fees, preventing Plaintiff from knowing any true “amount due.”
On or about March 26, 2024, Plaintiff was coerced under threat of eviction into signing an off‑site lease at different terms and higher rent, contrary to Michigan law and without meaningful consent.
On or about July 3, 2025, two individuals attempted to break into Plaintiff’s home and were captured on security cameras; those same individuals appeared during the later set‑out.

On July 17, 2025, with sheriff presence, locks were drilled, Plaintiff’s home was cleared, and property and appliances were removed or destroyed. Within 24 hours, a “FREE” sign was placed and neighbors took items; Defendant Brandel removed appliances and supplied a drill used to defeat locks.
Plaintiff is the lawful titleholder. The writ’s execution did not authorize non‑officers to destroy or distribute personal property, and no proper inventory/receipts were provided.

As a direct result, Plaintiff became homeless, slept in his car, and “unstable housing” was used in custody proceedings to suspend parenting time; Plaintiff has been deprived of contact with his son for approximately two months.
Defendants engaged in self‑help and unlawful interference by drilling locks, removing, retaining, and destroying Plaintiff’s personal property, and facilitating its distribution to third parties.
Such conduct violates MCL 600.2918 and entitles Plaintiff to statutory damages and other relief.
Defendants wrongfully exercised dominion over Plaintiff’s personal property, including appliances and household goods, inconsistent with Plaintiff’s rights.
Under MCL 600.2919a, Plaintiff is entitled to three times his actual damages, plus costs and reasonable attorney fees.
Defendants failed to maintain the premises in reasonable repair and fit for the use intended, including by permitting sewage conditions and failing to timely remediate while billing utilities.

Defendants imposed or sought to enforce terms and charges contrary to Michigan law, including unapproved utilities/fees and coerced lease changes, which are void and unenforceable under the Truth in Renting Act.
Defendants engaged in unfair, deceptive, and misleading practices by manipulating ledgers, refusing lawful payments, and misrepresenting balances and authority to coerce payment or eviction leverage.

Defendants breached duties of reasonable care by creating and failing to abate hazardous sewage conditions, mishandling property during execution activities, and disregarding legal limits on set‑outs.
Defendants’ extreme and outrageous conduct caused Plaintiff severe emotional distress, including homelessness, humiliation, grief, and loss of parenting time.
Defendants acted in concert to accomplish unlawful objectives or lawful objectives by unlawful means, including ledger manipulation, coerced leasing, and unlawful property interference.
Economic: replacement value of converted/destroyed property; repair/restoration of the home; alternative housing; out‑of‑pocket costs; lost earnings; litigation expenses.
Non‑economic: emotional distress, humiliation, and loss of enjoyment; collateral harm from suspended parenting time.
Statutory/exemplary: treble damages under MCL 600.2919a; statutory damages under MCL 600.2918; exemplary damages where permitted; fees and costs.
Enter judgment for Plaintiff on all Counts.
Award compensatory damages in an amount to be proven at trial.
Award treble damages under MCL 600.2919a and statutory damages under MCL 600.2918.
Award exemplary damages, costs, and reasonable attorney fees where permitted.
Enter declaratory and injunctive relief as justice requires, including correction of ledgers and compliance with law governing set‑outs and habitability.
Grant such other and further relief as the Court deems just and proper.
Exhibit A — Affidavit of Mitchell Shafer [PLACEHOLDER]
Exhibit B‑1 — EGLE Violation Letters/Notices [PLACEHOLDER]
Exhibit B‑2 — Sewage Complaint Email [PLACEHOLDER]
Exhibit C — Ledgers (Full, Unredacted) + Comparison Tables [PLACEHOLDER]
Exhibit C‑1 — Ledger/Balance Screenshots [PLACEHOLDER]
Exhibit D‑1 — Security Camera Photos (Attempted Break‑in ~2025‑07‑03) [PLACEHOLDER]
Exhibit D‑2 — Side‑by‑Side Identification (7/03 vs 7/17) [PLACEHOLDER]
Exhibit E — Witness Emails (Mitchell Shafer) [PLACEHOLDER]
Exhibit F — Authority Pack (MCR/MCL Excerpts) [PLACEHOLDER]
Exhibit F‑2 — Entity Sources (LARA printouts; HOA site; reporting) [PLACEHOLDER]
Exhibit G — Lease (PDF) [PLACEHOLDER]
Exhibit H — Coerced Lease Event Summary [PLACEHOLDER]
Exhibit I — Victim Impact Statement [PLACEHOLDER]
Exhibit J — Damages Workbook (CSV) [PLACEHOLDER]
Plaintiff demands trial by jury on all issues so triable.
I, Andrew J. Pigors, declare under penalty of perjury that the factual allegations made on personal knowledge in this Complaint are true to the best of my knowledge, information, and belief.
